entries.title: System context and responsibility boundary
entries.description: Flutter mobile and Next.js Admin consume a NestJS API. PostgreSQL owns durable truth; Valkey supports cache, presence, geospatial lookup, queues and socket scaling; workers own background processing; object storage holds controlled media and backup artifacts.
entries.audience: Infrastructure, Backend, Security, Finance, Operations and AI agents

entries.title: Production topology
entries.description: The provisional production baseline separates application, data and monitoring responsibilities and uses S3-compatible object storage. Exact network diagrams, region, sizing and failover require infrastructure approval before provisioning.
entries.audience: Infrastructure, Backend, Security, Finance, Operations and AI agents
keywords: Approved region, IP plan, firewall matrix, capacity test and failover design are not recorded.

entries.title: Staging topology
entries.description: Staging uses isolated infrastructure, sandbox provider credentials and synthetic data. It must never contain production user, KYC, payment or secret material.
entries.audience: Infrastructure, Backend, Security, Finance, Operations and AI agents
keywords: Private network availability, exact retention and environment access roster require approval.

entries.title: Network, DNS, TLS and secret boundaries
entries.description: Public entry points terminate TLS at controlled reverse proxies; data services remain private. Secrets belong in protected environment/secret storage and are never stored in this documentation repository.
entries.audience: Infrastructure, Backend, Security, Finance, Operations and AI agents
keywords: DNS registrar roles, TLS renewal ownership, firewall ports, VPN/private access and rotation schedule require explicit records.

entries.title: Monitoring, capacity and cost controls
entries.description: Monitor API/socket health, provider calls, payment reconciliation, queues, PostgreSQL, Valkey, backups and budget consumption. Alerts must name an owner and lead to a runbook.
entries.audience: Infrastructure, Backend, Security, Finance, Operations and AI agents
keywords: Final thresholds, paging rota, dashboards and capacity-test evidence require operational approval.

entries.title: Disaster recovery policy
entries.description: Recovery prioritizes data integrity, authoritative money state and controlled restoration. Off-server PostgreSQL backups and tested restore procedures are mandatory; server disks alone are not backups.
entries.audience: Infrastructure, Backend, Security, Finance, Operations and AI agents
keywords: Approved RPO, RTO, backup encryption/key ownership and regional recovery design require formal approval.

entries.description: Primary collection rail alongside Moniepoint.

entries.title: Task Owner, Tasker and Admin UAT
entries.description: Run complete role-based journeys with named representatives, record product gaps separately from defects and capture acceptance decisions.
entries.audience: QA, Mobile, Admin, Backend, Infrastructure, Operations, Product and AI agents

entries.title: Main flow
entries.description: A complete user or Admin journey with a clear beginning, outcome and next destination.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Subflow
entries.description: Reusable behavior used inside one or more main flows, such as OTP, media upload or permission recovery.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Contract
entries.description: The agreed request, response, event or error shape that connected systems must follow.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: OpenAPI
entries.description: A machine-readable description of the current HTTP API baseline; provisional gaps still block invention.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Data model
entries.description: A business record, its fields, ownership, relationships, privacy rules and persistence readiness.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Provider
entries.description: An external service such as payments, maps, identity verification, messaging or monitoring.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Build plan
entries.description: The approved delivery order for a team, separated into weeks and daily working outcomes.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Weekly pack
entries.description: One team-specific Markdown file containing Day 1 through Day 5 and the agent prompt for each day.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Release gate
entries.description: A required evidence checkpoint that must pass before an authorized Go/No-Go decision.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Canonical source
entries.description: The file that must be edited because it controls its generated outputs.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Generated output
entries.description: A reproducible reading or agent file that inherits authority from its canonical source and must not be edited directly.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Provisional
entries.description: Usable for review and planning but not permission to invent unresolved behavior, schema or policy.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Evidence
entries.description: A test result, reviewed artifact, trace, screenshot, log or approval that proves a requirement or gate.
entries.audience: Everyone

entries.title: Authority
entries.description: The approved source or qualified role that controls a decision when documents differ.
entries.audience: Everyone
